figures.caption: Figure 1: Per-task improvement over LAS (∆ = method accuracy − LAS accuracy) when steering each of the 11 tasks separately. Each subplot shows a different model. Each point shows the ∆ on a single task (colored by method). The diamond represents the average ∆ per method by averaging over all 11 tasks.
Figure 16: GSM8K-steered Llama-3.1-70B completions
Figure 3: Alpaca dataset prompt and evaluation templates
Figure 14: Alpaca-steered Llama-3.1-70B completions
Figure 4: Grandiloquent dataset prompt and evaluation templates

Table 8: Accuracy differences when steering toward ToxicChat on each of the 11 tasks versus the original model (∆ = steered − original accuracy). Each entry shows the accuracy difference of a method used for ToxicChat steering versus the original model on different tasks when using task-specific prompts. Positive values on −JailbreakBench indicate less jailbreak behavior. This table reports the per-task results when steering toward ToxicChat in Section 4.2.
Table 15: Concept monitoring accuracy for each of the 11 tasks. The accuracy for each task is the highest accuracy block (Max). This table reports the per-task results for Table 5 (Max).
Figure 18: JailbreakBench-steered Llama-3.1-70B completions
Figure 22: Repetition-steered Llama-3.1-70B completions
Figure 7: JailbreakBench dataset prompt and evaluation templates

Figure 2: Accuracy difference (∆) between the steered and original model (∆ = steered−original accuracy). Shown are Qwen2.5-7B (left) and Llama-3.1-8B (right) each steered toward one target task per subplot: GSM8K (top), ToxicChat (middle), JailbreakBench (bottom). Models steered toward each target task are evaluated on all 11 tasks. Each point shows the ∆ between the steered and original model for one of the 11 tasks (colored by method). The diamond represents the average ∆ per method across all 11 tasks.
Figure 24: ToxicChat-steered Llama-3.1-70B completions
Figure 6: IMDb dataset prompt and evaluation templates

Table 16: Mean and standard deviation of unsigned cosine similarities between per-block directions when aggregating over blocks, for each of the 11 tasks. Largest mean similarity per comparison is shown in bold.

Table 7: Accuracy differences when steering toward GSM8K on each of the 11 tasks versus the original model (∆ = steered − original accuracy). Each entry shows the accuracy difference of a method used for GSM8K steering versus the original model on different tasks when using task-specific prompts. Negative values on −JailbreakBench indicate more jailbreak behavior. This table reports the per-task results when steering toward GSM8K in Section 4.2.
Figure 11: Repetition dataset prompt and evaluation templates
Table 11: Accuracy averaged over 10 tasks (excludes JailbreakBench) when steering toward each task separately with rank-(1, 4, 8) ReFT and LoRA.

Table 12: Accuracy difference averaged over 11 tasks when steering toward one target task (GSM8K, ToxicChat, or JailbreakBench) with rank-(1, 4, 8) ReFT and LoRA versus the original model (∆ = steered − original accuracy). Positive values indicate better average than the original model and negative values indicate worse. This table extends Table 10 by including higher-rank results for ReFT and LoRA.
Figure 17: IMDb-steered Llama-3.1-70B completions

Table 6: Accuracies for all 11 tasks when steering toward each task separately. Non-Task Prompt is the accuracy of the original model using prompts that do not contain task-specific information. Task Prompt is the accuracy of the original model using prompts that contain task-specific information. CLAS, LoRA, ReFT, and LAS use the Non-Task Prompt. This table reports the per-task results for Section 4.1.
Figure 20: MMLU-steered Llama-3.1-70B completions
Table 5: Concept monitoring accuracy averaged over all 11 tasks. The accuracy for each task is based on the per-block accuracy averaged over all blocks (Avg) or the highest accuracy block (Max).

Table 1: Task and dataset summary. A task isolates a specific behavior from a source dataset. N probe/steer denote the number of training samples used for each dataset respectively. A ✓indicates the steer dataset uses o3-mini generated completions. The dataset prompts and evaluation templates are linked for each task.
Figure 12: TLDR dataset prompt and evaluation templates

Table 10: Accuracy difference averaged over 11 tasks when steering toward one target task (GSM8K, ToxicChat, or JailbreakBench) versus the original model (∆ = steered − original accuracy). Positive values indicate better average than the original model and negative values indicate worse. This table aggregates the results of Tables 7–9.
Figure 9: MMLU dataset prompt and evaluation templates
Figure 15: Grandiloquent-steered Llama-3.1-70B completions
Figure 5: GSM8K dataset prompt and evaluation templates
Figure 21: MNMT-steered Llama-3.1-70B completions
Figure 23: TLDR-steered Llama-3.1-70B completions
Figure 13: ToxicChat dataset prompt and evaluation templates
Figure 8: LeetCode dataset prompt and evaluation templates

Table 14: Concept monitoring accuracy for each of the 11 tasks. The accuracy for each task is the per-block accuracy averaged over all blocks (Avg). This table reports the per-task results for Table 5 (Avg).

Table 9: Accuracy differences when steering toward JailbreakBench on each of the 11 tasks versus the original model (∆ = steered − original accuracy). Each entry shows the accuracy difference of a method used for JailbreakBench steering versus the original model on different tasks when using task-specific prompts. This table reports the per-task results when steering toward JailbreakBench in Section 4.2.
